skill: Ratios, rates, proportional relationships, and units
stem: An object has a mass of 240 grams and a volume of 30 cubic centimeters. What is the density, in grams per cubic centimeter, of the object?
rationale: Choice D is correct. Density equals mass divided by volume: 240 grams ÷ 30 cubic centimeters = 8 grams per cubic centimeter. Choice A is incorrect because 210 = 240 - 30 results from subtracting volume from mass instead of dividing. Choice B is incorrect because 270 = 240 + 30 results from adding mass and volume. Choice C is incorrect because 7,200 = 240 × 30 results from multiplying mass by volume rather than dividing.
